issues_classified.description: The first-letter absorption proxy is degenerate: it returns exactly 0.0 on 26 of 27 GPT-2 checkpoints in E1 and 9 of 10 checkpoints in E3. A metric with near-zero variance cannot support claims about architectural tradeoffs or Pareto dominance.
issues_classified.suggestion: Integrate the full sae-spelling / SAEBench absorption pipeline into the E1/E3 code (exp/code/e1_full_gpt2.py and exp/code/e3_validation.py), or reframe E1 as a pipeline-validation study. Verify fix by running the updated pipeline on 5 checkpoints and confirming non-zero variance in absorption scores.

issues_classified.description: H3 is not merely unsupported—the task-agnostic metric is NEGATIVELY correlated with the first-letter benchmark (r = -0.592, p = 0.12). This suggests the two metrics measure different phenomena, not the same underlying construct.
issues_classified.suggestion: Rewrite the E3 Discussion section to frame this as a construct-validity problem rather than a weak negative result. Add a paragraph explicitly discussing whether 'absorption' as currently operationalized lacks cross-domain construct validity. Estimated effort: 30 minutes of editing.

issues_classified.description: E1's architecture comparison conflates families. The summary table compares only 'Standard' (n=23) vs. 'feature_splitting' (n=4), but the 27-checkpoint corpus includes TopK, TopK_MLP, TopK_Attn, and multiple hook-point variants lumped into 'Standard'.
issues_classified.suggestion: Regenerate the E1 summary table to show all distinct families/hook points (resid_pre, resid_post, resid_mid, mlp_out, attn_out, hook_z, feature_splitting). Report pairwise Mann-Whitney U tests for each family against the resid_pre baseline. Update the table in writing/sections/experiments.md and paper.md.

issues_classified.description: The pilot_summary.md explicitly rates metric quality as NO-GO for publication-ready numbers because the simplified absorption/hedging proxies are too crude and dead-neuron estimates are unreliable at 2k tokens. Yet the main paper presents these exact numbers as primary evidence.
issues_classified.suggestion: Add an explicit caveat in the E1 and E3 sections that these use simplified proxies. Better yet, replace the crude hedging proxy (5 antonym pairs) with a larger correlated-pair set (>= 50 pairs) and increase the activation corpus for dead-neuron detection to at least 50k tokens. Verify by re-running E1 and checking that dead-neuron estimates stabilize across 2k vs. 50k tokens.

issues_classified.description: The paper uses causal language ('causal cost,' 'unique causal effect') for E2, which is an observational meta-analysis. The section titles and abstract repeatedly use causal framing that overreaches the design.
issues_classified.suggestion: Replace 'causal' with 'predictive' or 'associational' in the abstract, Section 5 title, and Discussion. Keep the caveats, but do not lead with causal claims. Do a global search-and-replace pass in writing/sections/ and paper.md.

issues_classified.description: The Gemma-2-2B experiment (e1_full_gemma) failed due to gated HuggingFace access. The paper mentions this as a limitation but still frames the work as spanning 'Gemma-2-2B and Pythia-160M' in the abstract, giving readers the impression that modern models were directly evaluated in the controlled Pareto analysis.
issues_classified.suggestion: Clarify in the abstract that E1 (the controlled Pareto evaluation) is GPT-2 Small only, and that Gemma-2-2B appears only in the SAEBench meta-analysis (E2). Edit writing/sections/intro.md and paper.md abstract.

issues_classified.description: Figure 4 is introduced for the first time in the Discussion (Section 7.1) with no forward reference in any prior section. This breaks the paper's own convention and will confuse readers.
issues_classified.suggestion: Add a forward reference to Figure 4 at the end of Experiment 2 (e.g., 'Figure 4 visualizes the standardized regression coefficients and partial-correlation scatter for these relationships'). Edit writing/sections/experiments.md and paper.md.

issues_classified.description: Tables 1-3 are rendered as inline markdown tables without LaTeX labels or captions. The outline explicitly planned labeled LaTeX tables for NeurIPS submission.
issues_classified.suggestion: Convert all three inline tables into proper LaTeX table environments with \label{} and \caption{}. Ensure each table is referenced in the text before it appears. Update writing/latex/main.tex and regenerate the PDF.

issues_classified.description: Terminology is inconsistent: 'feature-splitting' vs. 'feature_splitting', 'CE recovered' vs. 'CE loss recovered' vs. '$\text{CE}_{\text{recovered}}$', 'JumpReLU' vs. 'JumpRelu'. TopK_MLP and TopK_Attn appear in E3 but are not defined in the glossary.
issues_classified.suggestion: Do a global alignment pass using glossary.md and notation.md as canonical sources. Use 'feature splitting' (no hyphen, no underscore), 'CE loss recovered' in prose, '$\text{CE}_{\text{recovered}}$' in math, and 'JumpReLU' everywhere. Add TopK_MLP and TopK_Attn definitions to writing/glossary.md.

issues_classified.description: The E3 pilot uses only 10 checkpoints and one hierarchy domain (geography). The paper acknowledges this but does not pre-register a replication protocol for expanding to 20-50 checkpoints and multiple domains.
issues_classified.suggestion: Add a short 'Replication Protocol' paragraph to the E3 section specifying exactly how future researchers would scale the task-agnostic metric to additional domains and checkpoints. Also, actually expand E3 to 20-30 checkpoints and 2-3 domains in the next iteration.

issues_classified.description: The pilot plan identified metric quality as NO-GO, yet the full experiment proceeded anyway. The planning stage failed to enforce a hard GO/NO-GO gate before scaling from pilot to full.
issues_classified.suggestion: Update the planning template/skill to require an explicit GO/NO-GO decision document before full-scale execution. If any pilot rating is NO-GO, the next stage must be metric/code repair, not scaling. This is a system-level process fix.

issues_classified.description: The novelty claim is strong and well-supported by the literature review. However, the empirical evidence backing the novelty is weaker than the claim due to proxy degeneracy and small sample sizes.
issues_classified.suggestion: Temper novelty claims to match the empirical scope: 'first systematic multi-objective evaluation on open-model checkpoints' rather than implying comprehensive coverage of all major architectures and models. Edit the abstract and conclusion.

success_patterns: E2 meta-analysis uses a large N=314 sample with appropriate statistical methods (partial correlation, OLS with cluster-robust SEs), providing the strongest empirical signal in the paper.
Honest reporting of negative results: E3's unsupported H3 is reported without spin, framed as a valuable negative result that raises questions about benchmark validity.
Strong integration of numbers and narrative: every key claim is anchored to a specific statistic (e.g., r_partial = -0.385, p < 0.001).
Clear conceptual reframing in the Conclusion: the paper synthesizes all three experiments into a crisp agenda shift from 'fixing absorption' to 'navigating unavoidable tradeoffs.'
The training-free design ensures ecological validity and low compute cost, which is appropriate for the project's constraints.
systemic_patterns: Pilot-to-full escalation gates are not being enforced. When a pilot rates something NO-GO, the system proceeds to full-scale execution anyway.
The training-free constraint forces narrow empirical coverage, but the paper's framing is not always scoped to match.
Writing quality checks (figure references, table labels, terminology alignment) are catching issues late, after the full draft is written.

suggestions: Add a pre-flight HF access check for gated models before scheduling experiments
Scale small pilots to their proposed N in a single batch when per-unit cost is negligible (E3 could have run 50 checkpoints in under 2 minutes)
Enforce a hard GO/NO-GO gate between pilot and full execution

recommended_focus: Replace the degenerate absorption proxy with the full sae-spelling / SAEBench pipeline
Reframe E3 negative correlation as a construct-validity problem
Fix all writing gaps (Figure 4 reference, LaTeX tables, terminology alignment)
Remove causal framing from E2 titles and abstract
